steps.n: These are special letters that begin some chapters of the Quran. We read them by their letter names: Kaf, Ha, Ya, Ayn, Sad. The small circles (ٓ) show where to pause when reading.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter. This word is the subject (doer) in this part of the verse.

steps.m: remembrance/mention (as the subject of this verse)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed from action (verb) to thing (noun) by changing middle vowel and adding ة at end
Added ِ at end to show it belongs to 'you' (when speaking to a female)
The doubled middle letter shows intensity, making it mean 'the complete nurturer/sustainer'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by رَحْمَتِ (mercy)
Added كَ to show it belongs to 'you' (masculine singular). This word is part of the phrase 'mercy of your Lord'
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'one who serves'. This word is receiving Allah's mercy (رَحْمَتِ) in this ayah.
Added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon by Allah's mercy
Added هُ at the end to show this servant belongs to Him (Allah)
This is the base part of the name, related to the concept of remembering or mentioning
Added ي with shadda to make it a name/proper noun from the root word meaning remembrance

steps.m: Zakariya (a proper name meaning 'God has remembered')
steps.n: Added آ at the end to make it a proper name. This is a common ending for Biblical names in Arabic.
This is a time word that shows something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects to the word نَادَىٰ (called) to show when he called.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long aa sound
The basic meaning of caring for or nurturing something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The middle letter is doubled with shadda to show intensity, making it mean 'one who nurtures completely'
Added هُ at the end to show possession (his). This word is being acted upon by the verb نَادَىٰ (called) in the verse.
The root letters that mean calling out
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel marks 'i' and 'a' to show the action of calling
Added hamza (ء) at the end and lengthened the alif to show it's a specific instance of calling
Added tanween to show this is the object of the verb نَادَىٰ (he called) in this ayah
The root letters that mean hiding or concealing
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'was hidden'
Adding shadda makes it an intensive adjective showing something is very hidden
Added tanween alif to show this describes how the calling was done - secretly/privately

steps.m: he said (introducing his words to رَبِّ/his Lord)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show it happened in the past. This word introduces what he said to his Lord.
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas, which is a common Arabic rule
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/plea to one's Lord
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English
The ن doubles and gets a kasra (ِ) to connect with the 'I' that will be attached to it, keeping the meaning 'indeed'
Adding ى at the end makes it about 'me'. This word connects to وَهَنَ (has become weak) to show who has become weak.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word describes what happened to العَظْمُ (the bone) in this ayah.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the hard part of the body. This word is what became weak in the phrase 'the bone became weak'.
Added the ُ ending to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: the bone (subject of وَهَنَ - became weak)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify we're talking about particular bones (the speaker's bones), not just any bones
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
The ن gets doubled (takes a shadda) when it connects with certain letters that come after it - similar to how we double letters in English words like 'swimming'
Adding ي with kasra at the end shows this means 'me'. Like changing 'from' to 'from me'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding اِ and تَ changes it to mean something happening by itself rather than someone doing it

steps.m: and it became intensely lit up (describes الرَّأْسُ - the head)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it a proper word. This word is what 'became white' (اشتعل) in the verse.
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ر letter to show it's a specific head being talked about
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action of becoming aflame
Changed the root into a noun representing the result of greying

steps.m: with grey hair (describing الرَّأْسُ - the head)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) to show it's describing something (the head) - like saying 'grey-haired'
Combines 'and' with 'not' to negate what comes after it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added أَ at start to show 'I' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: I am/exist (after لَمْ which makes it negative past)
steps.n: The word comes after لَمْ which makes it mean 'I was not'. This changes the word's form by removing the و and final ُ, replacing them with ۢ
Changed verb into noun showing the act of prayer
Extended the middle alif to show intensity of prayer
Added 'your' possession suffix
Added preposition 'bi' showing how something is done. This connects to the word شَقِيًّا (wretched) in the phrase.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures in a complete and perfect way
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Added vowels to make it mean 'he was unhappy' in the past
Changes from an action to describing someone's state

steps.m: utterly miserable (describing how he was with دُعَآئِكَ/your calling)
steps.n: Changed to end in ًا to show it describes how he felt when praying to Allah
This is a word that makes statements stronger and more certain
Added ي at the end to show this is about me
Added و at the start to connect this with what came before. This word connects to the verb خِفْتُ (I feared) that comes right after it.
Added short vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he feared' in the past

steps.m: I feared (referring to Prophet Zakariya expressing his concern)
steps.n: Adding تُ changes it to first person past tense (I)

steps.m: to protect, to be close to, to help

steps.n: Changes the root 'protect/help' into 'one who protects/helps' - like changing 'help' to 'helper'
Changes to make the plural: adds alif after م and changes the ending to ى

steps.m: the protectors (who Zakariya feared would mishandle inheritance after him)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific (the), and the ending َ shows this word is being acted upon by خِفْتُ (I feared) earlier in the verse
This is a basic preposition that connects two words by showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects 'relatives' (مَوٰلِىَ) with 'after me' (وَرَآءِى) to mean 'relatives after me'.
Added vowels and changed the final ي to ء to form the noun meaning 'behind'
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used as object of a preposition
Changed from 'behind me' to just 'behind' by removing the 'my' ending
Added ى to show it means 'my' or 'me'

steps.m: to be or exist (in any state or time)

steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding ت at the end shows the subject is feminine (like saying 'she' instead of 'he')
Adding kasra (ِ) at the end helps connect this word to the next word in the sentence

steps.m: and she was/existed (referring to امْرَأَتِى - my wife)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start connects this word to what came before. This word describes امْرَأَتِى (my wife) which comes right after it.
Root letters م-ر-أ got short 'a' vowels to make the basic word for 'woman'
Added 'i' sound at start and 'ah' sound at end to make the complete word for 'woman'
Changed ta marbuta to regular ta with kasra to show possession
Added ى to show first person possession (my) and changed initial alif to wasl form

steps.m: to be unable to have children
barren/unable to have children (as a description)
steps.n: Changes from verb (being barren) to describing someone who is barren

steps.m: barren (describes my wife in وَكَانَتِ ٱمْرَأَتِى)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end because this word comes after كَانَتِ (was) in the ayah, which requires this ending
The و is dropped when making a command form. Added a fatha on ه and sukun on ب to make the command form.

steps.m: so grant! (to Zakariya asking Allah for a child)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before (his fear about having no heir)
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'

steps.m: to/for me (connected to فَهَبْ - so grant)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to فَهَبْ (so grant), showing what should be granted to whom.
This is one of the most common Arabic prepositions. It shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with the word وَرَآءِى (after me) to show who he's talking about.
This word is usually used to mean 'from the presence of'
The vowels are added to make it a special word meaning 'from' or 'at'
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter
Added كَ to show it's 'your presence'. This comes after the word مِن (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be close to and protect
steps.n: The ي is added between the second and third root letters to create a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb showing a state or quality
Adding shadda makes it a noun describing someone who does this action strongly
The ًا shows this is what is being asked for in the prayer 'grant me a protector'

steps.m: to inherit or receive something from someone
he inherits (connected to يَعْقُوبَ in next ayah)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show 'he' is doing it now. The و from root drops because it's a weak letter. The ُ at the end shows this is the doer.
Added نِى at the end to show I am the one being inherited from. This connects to the next ayah where it mentions inheriting from the family of Jacob.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. The weak letter و changes to ي in present tense.
Added و to connect this word to the previous word يَرِثُنِى (inherits me/my inheritance). This shows a sequence of inheritance being described.
This is the basic form of the preposition without any vowel marks
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly: kasra (ِ) under م and sukun (ْ) on ن
The long alif (آ) is added to create the word meaning 'family'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it (family OF Jacob)
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a root - it's the name of the prophet Jacob. No morphological derivation needed since it's a borrowed proper name.
Added pronunciation marks to show how to say the name correctly: 'ya' with fatha, 'ayn' with sukun, 'qaf' with damma

steps.m: Jacob (as the object being inherited from)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يَرِثُ (inherits from). The fatha shows Jacob is being inherited from.

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif and sukun changes it from 'he made' to 'make!' (a command)
Added هُ to show who is being made/set into something
Added وَ (and) at the start

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop something to its completion

steps.n: Doubling of middle letter ب intensifies meaning
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
Added vowels and the weak letter و changed to ي to show past tense. This is a common change with و at the end of words.
Adding shadda (ّ) makes the quality stronger and changes it from an action to a description

steps.m: very pleasing (as a quality of something/someone)
steps.n: Adding ًا shows this word is describing something in the sentence. In this ayah, it's describing how Allah wants the heir to be.
This is the base name Zakariyya, which comes from Hebrew meaning 'God has remembered'
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we're calling out to Zakariyya, like saying 'Hey Zakariyya!' or 'O Zakariyya!'
The alif at the end is lengthened to show a pause in speech, which is common when calling out to someone
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes the good news about to be given to Zakariya.

steps.m: Indeed we (subject of نُبَشِّرُكَ - 'we give good news')
steps.n: Added ا ('we') to إِنَّ ('indeed') to show that Allah is speaking and giving the good news
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more intensive
The نُ at the start means 'we' are doing the action now
The كَ shows who receives the good news - it means the news is being given to 'you'
This pattern is used for young ones - here it means a young male
Added ٍ at the end to show it's indefinite (like saying 'a' young boy instead of 'the' young boy)
Added بِ at the start to show 'with' or 'by means of'
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make the past tense verb 'he named'
Changed from 'to name' verb to 'name' noun by dropping letters and adding اِ at start
Changed initial alif to hamzat wasl and added damma to show this is the subject
Added هُ to show possession - this refers to Yahya (يَحْيَىٰ) who is mentioned right after this word

This is a proper name derived from the verb 'to live'. The ي at the beginning shows it's like a present/future tense verb ('he lives/will live'), while the ى at the end is a special ending for proper names.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the final ى to show it's a proper name, not just a verb. This doesn't change the meaning but helps with proper reading.
This is a helper word that makes verbs talk about something that didn't happen in the past. Here it works with يَحْيَىٰ to say there was no one named Yahya before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: we make (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The نَ at the start shows Allah is speaking as 'We', and the sukun (ْ) on ع shows this is happening now or in the future
This is a preposition showing purpose or direction, connected to the verb نَجْعَل (we made/assigned)
The letter lam gets stronger (doubled) to add emphasis, changing 'for/to' into 'surely/definitely for/to'
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from in time or space. Here it combines with قَبْلُ (before) to mean 'from before'
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word connects to what comes after it
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Adding damma at the end shows this word is in the nominative case

steps.m: namesake/one who shares the same name
steps.n: The doubled letter at the end makes it mean 'someone characterized by having a name'
Adding the double fatHa makes it indefinite and shows it's being used as an object
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word shows who is speaking before saying رَبِّ (my Lord).
The و (weak letter) changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does this action completely - the ultimate nurturer
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
This is the base form meaning 'that/indeed'
These are actually two different words - أَنَّىٰ is a separate question word meaning 'how', not derived from أَنَّ (that). They just look similar but have different roots and meanings.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he becomes/will be (questioning how غُلٰمٌ/a son could exist)
steps.n: The يَ at the start shows this happens now or in the future. The ُ (damma) marks show it's happening on its own. This word is part of Zakariya questioning how he could have a son.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for me, to me (connected to يَكُونُ - will be)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to يَكُونُ (will be) to show what will be 'for me'.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'youth'
Added tanween (double damma) at the end to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a boy' instead of 'the boy')
The root letters were joined together and given vowel marks. The middle letter و became ا to make it easier to say with the fathas.

steps.m: she was (referring to امْرَأَتِى - my wife)
steps.n: Added ت to show the subject is feminine (referring to 'my wife' in this ayah)
Added kasra at the end to help connect with the next word (امْرَأَتِى - my wife)

steps.m: and she was (connecting to previous statement about having a son)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this statement about his wife being barren to his previous question about having a son
Basic root letters of 'woman' with vowel marks added to make it readable
Adds letters to make the common noun 'woman'
Changed ة to ت to show this word is connected to something after it
Added ى at the end to show this is 'my' wife

steps.m: to be unable to bear children
barren/unable to bear children (as a description)
steps.n: Changes from verb (being barren) to describing someone who is barren
Adds ًا at the end because it describes 'my wife' after the word 'was' (كَانَتِ)
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This particle emphasizes the certainty of the verb that follows it (بَلَغْتُ - I have reached).

steps.m: and indeed/and already (emphasizes the verb بَلَغْتُ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
Added vowels (َ َ َ) to make it 'he reached' in past tense

Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. The sukun (ْ) is added before تُ to help pronunciation. In this ayah, this word connects to مِنَ ٱلْكِبَرِ (from old age) to show what the speaker reached.
This is the basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or being part of something
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word الْكِبَرِ (old age). This shows the connection between 'from' and what it's coming from.
Changes from verb 'to grow big' to noun meaning 'state of being old'
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)

steps.m: to be advanced in age/exceed limits

steps.n: Added kasra vowels and intensive يّ ending to show extreme degree of being advanced

steps.m: extremely advanced in age (describing بَلَغْتُ - I have reached)
steps.n: Added ًا ending to show this describes how far he has reached in age, connecting to the verb بَلَغْتُ (I have reached) in the ayah

steps.m: he said (subject of هُوَ عَلَىَّ هَيِّنٌ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense. This word shows what was said before هُوَ عَلَىَّ هَيِّنٌ (it is easy for Me).
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Starting with the comparison particle ك that shows similarity
Added ذا which is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this'
Added ل with kasra to make the word point to something far away ('that') instead of close by ('this')

steps.m: like that (referring to what was just said in قَالَ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is referring back to what was just mentioned in the previous word قَالَ (he said)
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is what رَبُّكَ (your Lord) said.
The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Doubling the ب shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The ُ shows this word is doing the action in قَالَ (he said)
Added كَ to show it's your Lord. This word is doing the action in قَالَ (he said)
This is a pronoun meaning 'he'. It doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it refers to creating something (making a son for Maryam) being easy for Allah.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'

steps.m: upon me (referring to creating something, as mentioned by اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added ي (me) at the end, which combines with the ى to form a shadda. This shows the preposition is acting on 'me'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The و changes to ي because it comes between two fathas
Changed from a verb (became easy) to an adjective (easy) by adding marks - double ي with kasra, and tanween at end
قد is a particle that adds emphasis or certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes Allah's previous creation.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. The sukun (ْ) on د shows the word is complete.

steps.m: to create from nothing, to bring into existence

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he created' in the past tense
Added sukun (ْ) on ق to prepare for adding suffixes
Added تُ to show 'I' did the action
The كَ suffix shows who was created (you). This connects to the previous ayah where Allah is speaking to Zakariya.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to قَبْلُ (before) to mean 'from before'.
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to create the basic noun meaning 'before'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject in the sentence
This is a negative particle that makes present tense verbs mean past tense. It's used with the verb تَكُ (to be) in this ayah to say something was not true in the past.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects to تَكُ (to be) to say 'and you were not'.
Changed the root letters to make the past tense 'he was' by adding fathas and dropping the middle و
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' or 'she', changed vowels to show present tense
The word changes its ending from تَكُونُ to تَكُنْ because لَمْ makes verbs shorter and changes their meaning to 'did not'
Final ن is dropped in this common shortened form, but meaning stays the same
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun. This word is the object of تَكُ (you were) in the phrase 'you were not a thing'.
Added tanween alif to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon. In this context, it means 'anything at all' - emphasizing complete non-existence.

steps.m: he said (introducing his words رَبِّ ٱجْعَل لِّىٓ ءَايَةً)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense. This word introduces what Zakariya said to his Lord.
The middle letter و changes to ا, which is a common change for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely, making it mean 'Lord'
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding ٱ at the start and changing the vowels. This is Zakariya asking Allah to make/give him a sign.
Basic preposition showing purpose or direction
The shadda adds emphasis to show importance

steps.m: surely for me (asking for a sign from اللَّهِ)
steps.n: The ى shows this is happening to or for 'me' - in this ayah, Zakariya is asking Allah for a sign

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear
steps.n: Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters

steps.m: a sign or miracle (something that shows or appears)
steps.n: The root letters change to make a noun meaning 'one sign/miracle'. The middle letter becomes longer (ا) and we add ة at the end.
Added tanween fatha (ً) to show this is the object being made into a sign in the phrase 'make for me a sign'
Changed from basic root 'say' to 'he said' by adding vowels. Shows someone speaking to their Lord.
The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Connected the root letters with vowels and changed the first letter to hamza with fatha. The middle weak letter becomes alif.
Adds feminine marker to make it a feminine noun

steps.m: your sign (referring to the miracle given to Zechariah)
steps.n: The word 'sign' gets 'your' added to it (the كَ at the end). The ـُ shows this is the main thing being talked about in the sentence.
Starting with the word أَنْ which introduces a new clause or statement
Added لَا (not) after أَنْ to negate what comes after

steps.m: that not (subject of تُكَلِّمَ - you speak)
steps.n: The ن from أَنْ combines with the ل from لَا, creating a doubled ل sound (shown by the ّ mark). This is just a pronunciation change, not a meaning change.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding a shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the speaking more direct or intensive

steps.m: that you speak (in the context of أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ - that you will not speak)
steps.n: Added تُ at the beginning to show 'you' and changed the middle vowel to kasra to show it's happening now or in the future. This word comes after أَلَّا (that not) to mean 'that you will not speak'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who is social/people (since humans are social beings)
steps.n: Changes from 'he was social' to 'people' by dropping the first letter أ and adding ا after ن. This is how we get the word for 'people' from the idea of being social.
Adds ال at the beginning to point to specific people, and doubles the ن with shadda

steps.m: the people (being acted upon by تُكَلِّمَ - you speak to)
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end to show these are the people being spoken to (object of the verb 'speak')
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Adding alif to make it a number noun
The alif is written as a small mark above (dagger alif) instead of a full letter. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters get vowels to make the word for 'night'
Special plural form for 'night' - changes the middle of the word
Changes to special plural form لَيَالٍ when counting nights (3-10), with a special ending sound

steps.m: to be straight, balanced, or complete

steps.n: Adds vowels to make an adjective describing something perfect or complete
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning
Added tanween alif to show this describes how something is done

steps.m: he went out (from المحراب to قومه)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, it shows Zakariya went out from the prayer chamber (المحراب) to his people (قومه).

steps.m: so he went out (from المحراب to قومه)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of something before it
This is the basic form of the word that carries the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى with alif on top (ىٰ) turns this into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of or related to another. Here it shows how خَرَجَ (he came out) relates to قَوْمِهِ (his people).
The root meaning of standing together becomes a word for a group of people who stand as one. This is the basic noun form.
Adding kasra shows this word is being possessed by someone or follows certain words
Adding هِ shows the people belong to him (referring to the subject of the verse)
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: from (connects to ٱلْمِحْرَابِ - the prayer niche)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this preposition is connected to the word after it (ٱلْمِحْرَابِ). This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to struggle or strive against something

steps.n: Basic vowels (fatha) added to make it past tense 'he struggled'
Added مِ at start to show place, and ا in middle to show importance. Changed meaning from action to place where action happens.
Added ٱلْ to show this is a specific sanctuary, and ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added أ at start to show causing someone else to receive the communication. The و gets a sukun mark.

steps.m: then he revealed/inspired (to قَوْمِهِ - his people)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened after he came out to his people

steps.m: then he revealed/inspired (to قَوْمِهِ - his people)
steps.n: Added alif maksura (ىٰ) and elongation mark (ٓ) for proper pronunciation
Basic preposition showing direction or movement toward something
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to them (who he signaled to in فَأَوْحَىٰٓ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this is directed to a group of people. In this ayah, it refers back to the people he signaled to in the word فَأَوْحَىٰٓ (then he signaled).
This is a basic particle that connects parts of a sentence, often introducing what someone said, did, or was told to do. Here it introduces what he gestured to them to do (سَبِّحُوا - glorify).
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. The word introduces what he gestured to them - that they should glorify Allah.

steps.m: to glorify, to praise, to swim

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he glorified' to 'you glorify!' by adding a shadda (double letter) and changing the ending - this makes it a command
Added plural command ending to make it address a group

steps.m: to come/do early in the morning

steps.n: Changes from a verb about morning actions into the word for early morning time
Added ة to show one specific morning or instance of morning time
Added ً (tanween) to show this describes when something happened

Added ًا to show this describes when something happens
Adds conjunction و meaning 'and'
Formed the proper name Yahya by adding vowel marks and changing the final ي to ى. This name is derived from the meaning 'he lives' or 'may he live'.
Added يٰ at the beginning to create the vocative form for calling out to someone named Yahya

steps.m: O Yahya! (calling out to Prophet Yahya)
steps.n: Added the elongation mark (ٰ) at the end to complete the vocative form. This appears in a verse where Allah is commanding Prophet Yahya to take the scripture with determination.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to command form by: 1) removing first letter, 2) adding damma on first remaining letter, 3) adding sukun on second letter

steps.m: take! (command to one male) (connected to الْكِتٰبَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word الْكِتٰبَ (the book) which is the thing being taken
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha
Changed from 'wrote' (verb) to 'book' (noun) - the thing that was written. This book is what is being taken in this ayah.
Added ٱلْ to show this is 'the book' specifically
Root meaning 'strong' became a noun meaning 'one strength/power' by adding ّ (doubling) and ة at the end
Added بِ to show how the action should be done. This connects to the command 'take' (خُذِ) earlier in the verse.
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite and is being acted upon by the command 'take' (خُذِ).
Adding alif at beginning changes meaning from 'come' to 'give/bring to someone'. The word الْحُكْمَ (wisdom) is what's being given.
Adding ي with sukoon changes it from a verb to an intensive doer
Adding نا at the end shows that 'we' did the action
Adding هُ shows who received the action (him)
Adding و connects this to the previous statement
Added ُ and ْ to change from an action to the special knowledge that comes from making good judgments. This word is what was given (object of ءَاتَيْنٰهُ - we gave him)
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the wisdom' instead of just 'wisdom'). Added َ at the end because this wisdom is being given to Yahya - it's the object of the verb 'we gave him'
Added vowels and changed و to ي to make the basic word for 'young'
Added shadda (double letter) at the end to make it mean 'a child' instead of just 'childlike'
Added tanween and alif to show this is the object being given in the phrase 'We gave him wisdom as a child'
Added vowels and alif to create a noun expressing a strong feeling
Added -an ending to show this is a special gift from Allah

steps.m: and tender compassion (as a divine gift)
steps.n: Added و to connect this divine gift with other blessings mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added shadda to show strong connection with the next word لَّدُنَّا (from Us). Together they mean 'from Our presence'
The vowels change the word to mean 'from' or 'near to'
The doubled letter adds emphasis to show it's directly from someone's presence
Added نَا (naa) at the end to mean 'Our', showing it's from Allah's presence

steps.m: to purify, make pure and good

Adds 'and' to connect this word with what came before it
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's being used as an object
Added fatha marks to show past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: to guard against harm or evil
one who guards against evil, pious
steps.n: ت at start shows this describes someone who does the action
Adding ًا shows this word is describing someone or something in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this quality is very strong
The ًا ending shows this word is describing the subject of يَكُن (he was) in the ayah
Adds 'and' to connect this description with other qualities mentioned
This pattern shows someone who does the action - someone who gives birth or begets children
Adding َيْ makes it refer to exactly two parents
Adding هِ shows these are his parents

steps.m: to/towards his two parents (object of بَرًّا)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows direction or relationship. This word is connected to بَرًّا (dutiful) in the ayah, showing he was dutiful to his parents.

Adding وَ connects this negative statement to the previous phrase وَبَرًّۢا بِوٰلِدَيْهِ (and dutiful to his parents)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action
The word لَمْ before this word causes it to change to this shorter form and changes the meaning to past negative (was not)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who forces others a lot (tyrant)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (ب) with the alif after it shows someone who does this action a lot

steps.m: a tyrant (object of يَكُن - was not)

Added vowels and doubled the ي to show this describes someone who strongly disobeys
Added ًا to show this word describes what kind of person he was not (connected to يَكُن/was not in the ayah)

Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it a proper word
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُبْعَثُ (is raised)
Added و at beginning to connect this to the previous phrase
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed first vowel to 'oo' sound to make it a command to someone, telling them to die

Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning a single day
Added fatha to show this word is receiving the action of يُبْعَثُ (is raised)

steps.m: to raise up or send forth

Changed from active to passive by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show something is being raised up instead of doing the raising

steps.m: Mary (as the subject being mentioned in ٱذْكُرْ)

The kasra (ِ) is added because this word connects to the verb that comes after it (انتَبَذَتْ meaning 'she withdrew')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb

The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity of the quality
Adding double damma makes it 'an easy thing' instead of just 'easy'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he made' to 'we make' by adding ن (na) at the start
Added لِ (lām) at the beginning to indicate purpose/intent ('so that')
Added conjunction و (waw) to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and so that we make him
steps.n: Added pronoun هُۥٓ meaning 'him' to show who is being made/set. The ۥٓ marks are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.
The first letter changes to ء with fatha, and the first ي becomes ا to make it easier to pronounce
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of a sign

steps.m: a sign, a miracle (object of لِنَجْعَلَهُ)
steps.n: Adding tanween (ً) shows this is the object being made into a sign in the phrase 'We will make him a sign'
The hamza is dropped and a long alif is added after first letter to create the noun meaning 'people'
Adding ال makes it specific people rather than just any people. The ن gets a shadda when ال is added.
Adding لِ shows this is for or to the people. This word comes after آيَةً (sign) to show who the sign is for.
The first ل combines with the second ل to make a stronger L sound (shown by the shadda), and the word gets a kasra ending

steps.m: to show kindness and care for someone

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to be kind' into a noun meaning 'kindness' by adding specific vowels
Adding ة at the end makes it refer to one specific instance of kindness
Added و (and) at the start and ً at the end to show it's connected to the word آيَةً (sign) that came before it in the verse
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' that shows where something comes from or its source
Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'. The ن of مِن combines with the ن of نا, shown by the ّ (shadda) mark
Added vowels to make it past tense and show who did the action
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of verb. This makes it easier to say.

steps.m: and he was (connected to هُوَ عَلَىَّ هَيِّنٌ - it is easy for Me)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about something being easy for Allah

steps.m: a matter or decision (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Changed the action into a thing by adding fatha and sukun. This word is what was being referred to by كَانَ (it was) in this ayah.
Added an alif at the end to show this is receiving the action (like saying 'a matter' instead of just 'matter')
Added vowels to connect the root letters. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Changed to show what was decreed rather than who did the decreeing
Added emphasis by doubling the final letter
Added the ًا ending to show this describes what was decreed (in this case, أَمْرًا - a matter)
Added extra emphasis with shadda on initial م

steps.m: to carry/bear (like a mother carrying a child)

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding a-a vowels, means 'he carried'
Adding ت at the end makes it feminine
The ه at the end means 'him'. This is a verb with an attached pronoun, not a noun that would have case endings.

steps.m: so she became pregnant with him (referring to Jesus)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happened as a result of what came before. This specifically refers to Mary becoming pregnant with Jesus.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Added اِنْتَ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself
Added ت at the end to show a female subject

steps.m: and so she withdrew herself (after حَمَلَتْهُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this happened after she became pregnant (حَمَلَتْهُ)

steps.m: with him (referring to the pregnancy/conception mentioned in فَحَمَلَتْهُ)
steps.n: Added the vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word. The بِ means 'with' and the هِ means 'him/it', referring back to the pregnancy mentioned in the previous word فَحَمَلَتْهُ (she carried/conceived it).

steps.m: with him (referring to the pregnancy/conception mentioned in فَحَمَلَتْهُ)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after the هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens, and added ا after و to make it a noun

steps.m: to a place (that she withdrew to)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the place she withdrew to, connecting it to the verb انتبذت (she withdrew) in the ayah
Added vowels and ي to make it into a word meaning 'very far'. The doubled ّ at the end makes the meaning stronger.
Added ًا at the end to show this describes a place that is being talked about in the sentence. This word describes مَكَانًا (a place) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels and turning middle letter ي into ا
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes the meaning from 'he came' to 'he made someone/something come'

steps.n: Adding ف shows this happened after something else

steps.m: and then labor pains came to her (subject of المَخَاضُ)
steps.n: Added ها to show it happened to her, and lengthened the alif to show emphasis. This word connects to المَخَاضُ (labor pains) which is the subject doing the action.

steps.m: to shake violently or churn (like churning milk or experiencing strong movement)
labor pains (the strong movements and pain during childbirth)
steps.n: Changes the concept of shaking/churning into the specific pain and movement felt during childbirth

steps.m: the labor pains (that came to مَرْيَمَ)
steps.n: Adding ال shows these are the specific labor pains that came to Mary, not just any labor pains

steps.m: the labor pains (that drove مَرْيَمَ to the palm tree)

Added alif maksura with a small alif above (ىٰ) at the end. This shows it's connecting to the word after it (جِذْعِ meaning trunk).

Added kasra at the end to show this trunk belongs to or is connected to the palm tree mentioned next

Adding ة at the end makes it mean one palm tree instead of palm trees in general
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: to/towards the palm tree (connected to إِلَىٰ)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word follows إِلَىٰ (to/towards) in the ayah

Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female (Maryam) is speaking. This word connects to يٰلَيْتَنِى (Oh I wish) which shows what she said.
This is the basic form of the word used to express wishes or regrets about things that can't happen
Adding يٰ at the start makes it into a call of distress or strong emotion

steps.m: Oh, if only I (said by Maryam expressing regret)
steps.n: Adding نِى at the end shows this is about the speaker (Maryam) herself

Added alif with overbar (ٰ) which is a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: and I was (connected to مِتُّ - I died)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this phrase to the previous word مِتُّ (I died), showing these actions happened together
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create a noun meaning the result of forgetting

steps.m: a forgotten thing (object of وَكُنتُ - I was)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is something being acted upon. This word is the object of وَكُنتُ (I was) in this ayah.

steps.m: to forget or leave something behind

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'
Changed to show something that was forgotten rather than someone who forgot
Doubling the م makes the meaning stronger - emphasizes how thoroughly forgotten something is
Added tanwin alif to show this is describing the word نسيا in the verse
Added vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he called out'
Added alif after first root letter to show emphasis in the action

steps.m: and then he called to her

Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Adding kasra at the end makes it work as a preposition

Starting with أَنْ which introduces a new clause or statement

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter ب shows this is someone who does this action perfectly and completely
Adding damma shows this is the main subject being talked about
Adding كِ shows this is speaking to a female (Maryam) about her Lord
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'under'
Added كِ to show this means 'beneath you' when speaking to a female. This is referring to Maryam in the ayah.
Root letters connected with vowels to make 'flowing' (like water moving)
Added shadda to intensify the meaning
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah
Adding vowel marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: by/with the trunk (of النَّخْلَةِ - the palm tree)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show how to use the trunk, and ِ at the end because it's connected to another word
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun for palm trees in general
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific palm tree
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific palm tree that was mentioned

steps.m: of the palm tree (possessed by جِذْعِ/trunk)

Added تُ at start to show 'she/it' (the palm tree), changed middle vowel, and made it present tense 'makes fall'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to another word
Added كِ which means 'you (female)' to show who is being addressed
Changes from verb 'to be ripe' into noun meaning 'ripe dates'

steps.m: fresh ripe dates (that will fall upon you)
steps.n: The ًا (tanween) at the end shows this is the object - the thing being dropped. It tells us these fresh dates are what will fall down.
The vowels change the root into an adjective describing something fresh
Added shadda to intensify the meaning of freshness
Added tanween alif to show this word describes the dates (رُطَبًا) mentioned in the ayah
Forms the basic command by dropping the first letter and adding damma and kasra vowels
Adds ي to make the command feminine, addressing a female

Added ي at the end to make it feminine command
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Doubling the ر adds emphasis to the meaning of settling/contentment
The kasra changes it from a statement to a command
Added ي to make it feminine command
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', connecting it to previous commands in the verse (eat and drink)

steps.m: then if ever (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Adding فَ connects this condition to what was said before, showing it follows from the previous statement about eating and drinking

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

Added نَّ at the end to show emphasis and certainty

Adds ًا to show this word receives the action in the sentence

steps.n: Added tanween alif (ـًا) to show this is the object being brought. The ء changes to ٔ when it has tanween.

steps.m: to give, to bring, to come
