steps.n: Added alif at beginning to show Allah is causing the blessing to happen. The word relates to عَلَيْهِم (upon them) showing who receives the blessing.
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and absoluteness of Allah

steps.m: Allah (The One True God) (subject of أَنْعَمَ - bestowed favors)
steps.n: Added ُ to show Allah is the one doing the action (bestowing favors) in this verse. The alif at the start is written as ٱ when connecting to previous words.
The base preposition عَلَى changes its final ى to ي when connecting to pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِم at the end to show this applies to multiple people (them). This هِم refers back to the people mentioned before in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ (those) who were blessed.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. The kasra helps connect it to words after it.
The shadda emphasizes the م, making the word stronger.
The fatha at the end helps connect this word to the next word (the prophets) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic noun
Forms intensive noun meaning 'one who brings important news/revelations'
Added 'the' to make it definite and doubled the ن with shadda
Added plural ending ين (written as ۦنَ) to show multiple prophets

steps.m: from/among (connecting النَّبِيِّۦنَ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing that what follows it (the prophets) is part of a larger group mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that the blessed people being discussed are specifically from among the prophets.

steps.m: to spread or scatter (like seeds or descendants)

steps.n: Makes it into a noun meaning 'those who are scattered/spread'
The ِ at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something
The kasra ending shows this word belongs to/is connected to Ibrahim
Changes the root into the proper name Adam. The long 'aa' sound (ا) makes it a special name.

steps.m: Adam (as the object being talked about)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows that Adam is being talked about as part of a list of prophets mentioned in this verse
This is the basic question word that asks about people, like 'who' in English. The sukun shows where the word stops.
Added the word مِنْ which means 'from' before مَنْ
When مِنْ comes before مَنْ, the ن from مِنْ combines with the م from مَنْ. We show this by removing the ن and adding ّ (shadda) on the م.
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters together
Added fatha vowels to make past tense singular form
Changed final vowel to sukun to prepare for suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a preposition that shows accompaniment. In this ayah, it connects to نُوحٍ (Noah) to show who was carried along with others.
This is the proper name of the prophet Noah, which comes from a root meaning related to lamenting or crying out. The و here is part of the name's structure.
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end shows this is part of a possessive phrase, indicating something belongs to or comes from Noah. In this ayah, it's talking about the descendants (ذُرِّيَّةِ) of Noah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added ي after first root letter to create noun form. Also added damma (ُ) on first letter and shadda on second root letter for emphasis
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense form
Changed first vowel to 'u' sound and doubled middle letter to show lots of scattered things
Added ي with shadda at the end to make it mean 'related to offspring/descendants' (like adding -ish or -y in English)
Added ة to make it a noun referring to a group
Added kasra at the end to show possession by Adam, mentioned in the ayah
This is a proper noun, the name of the prophet Abraham. It's a non-Arabic name that was Arabized from the original Hebrew/Aramaic name.

steps.m: Abraham (mentioned in list with other prophets)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this name is part of a list being mentioned. It comes after the word ذُرِّيَّةِ (descendants of) in this verse.
This is a proper noun referring to Prophet Jacob (يعقوب). The name combines two ancient words meaning 'servant/wrestler' and 'God'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this name to the previous names mentioned in the verse
This is the base relative pronoun used to refer to people
Added the preposition مِنْ (from) before the relative pronoun
The ن of مِنْ combines with م of مَنْ to form a doubled letter with shadda (مّ)
Added the conjunction وَ (and) to connect this phrase with what came before
This is the basic concept of guiding or showing the way
Changed from basic root to past tense verb by adding fathas and writing ى at the end
The ى changes back to ي with sukun when adding a suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added letters to make it mean 'specially chose for himself' - like picking something special just for you
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', connecting this with the previous statement
Changed spelling: hamza becomes waṣla (ٱ) after و, and lengthened final alif for pause
This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects two actions: when something is recited (تُتْلَىٰ), people do something in response.
Connected the root letters together
Added تُ at start and changed vowels to make it passive (something is being recited rather than someone reciting)
The word gets ى at the end to show it's happening to something feminine (like 'she' or 'it')
The small alif (ٰ) extends the sound of the ى. This word refers to آيَاتُ (verses) in this ayah.
This is a common preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else
Added ي with sukun (يْ) to connect to the upcoming pronoun

steps.m: upon them (connected to أَنْعَمَ - bestowed favors)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this affects multiple people (them). This word connects to أَنْعَمَ (bestowed favors) in the ayah, showing who received Allah's favors.
This root relates to things becoming clear or obvious
Changes from a root meaning 'clear' into a noun meaning 'sign' by adding special marks
Changes the word to plural form
Adds damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence. This word is the subject of خَرُّوا (they fell down)
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Forms an intensive adjective indicating complete/perfect mercy
Adding ٱل (al) makes it specific - this isn't just any extremely merciful one, but THE Most Merciful, referring to Allah
The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to the word ءَايٰتُ (verses) - like saying 'verses of the Most Merciful'
The doubled ر (shadda) emphasizes the suddenness and force of falling

steps.m: they fell down prostrate suddenly/forcefully (when hearing Allah's verses)
steps.n: Changes from singular 'he fell' to plural 'they fell'. This describes how the blessed people mentioned earlier responded when hearing Allah's verses.

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down with forehead touching ground

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he prostrated' to 'prostrators' by doubling middle letter and changing vowels to make plural noun from verb
Added tanween fatHa to show this describes the state or condition of the people mentioned in the previous part of the verse (خَرُّوا۟ - they fell down)
Changed from 'to weep' into 'being wept upon' - like when someone is so sad others cry over them
Added tanween (double marks) at the end to show this is describing how they wept when prostrating

steps.m: and weeping (describing their state during prostration)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with سُجَّدًا (prostrating) - they fell prostrating and weeping
Basic past tense verb showing someone succeeded or came after others. This word is connected to أَضَاعُوا (they wasted) as its subject.
Added فَ at the beginning to show contrast with what came before - this is a negative succession unlike the previous generations mentioned
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the verb خَلَفَ (came after) with بَعْدِهِمْ (after them) to show who came after whom.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to the root letters to make the word mean specifically 'after'

steps.m: after them (referring to the previous people mentioned)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'after them'. The 'them' refers back to the previous people mentioned in the ayah.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning someone/something that comes after
Added tanween damma to show this is an indefinite subject. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb خَلَفَ (succeeded/came after) in this ayah.
The middle letter ي changed to ا (alif) to make the past tense 'he wasted'. Like many Arabic verbs with ي in the middle.
Added أ at beginning to show causing something to happen. This changes it from just losing something to actively making it become lost/wasted.

steps.m: they made (something) be lost/wasted (subject of خَلَفَ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did this action. This word describes what the bad successors (خَلْفٌ) did.
Connected the root letters with short vowels (fat-ha) to make a basic word for prayer
Added ة to make it a specific instance of prayer
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which joins with ص to make it stronger
Changed و to ٰ for standard spelling of this word
Added fatha to show this is the object of the verb أَضَاعُوا۟ (they neglected)

steps.m: to follow or go after something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added اِ at start and doubled the ت to show this is done with extra effort or purpose
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Added vowel marks and ة to make it mean one specific instance of desire
Changed to feminine plural form to show multiple desires
Added ال to make it definite and shadda on ش because it comes after ال
The word changed to show it's being acted upon (someone is following these desires). The alif was written with a special mark (ٰ) which is common in the Quran
Basic word that shows something will definitely happen in the future, stronger than just 'will'

steps.m: so/then they will (consequence of أَضَاعُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ - neglecting prayer)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a direct consequence of them neglecting prayer and following desires. فَ here indicates a stronger and more immediate connection than و would.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show present/future tense. The final ي changed to ى because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: they meet/encounter (referring to those who wasted prayer)
steps.n: Added وْنَ to show multiple people doing the action. This word refers to those mentioned earlier who wasted prayer.

steps.m: to deviate from the right path

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'going astray' to a noun meaning 'straying/deviation' by adding vowels
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity

steps.m: severe deviation (object of يَلْقَوْنَ - they will meet)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha and alif to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يَلْقَوْنَ (they will meet)

steps.m: except (introduces an exception to the previous statement)
steps.n: This is a fundamental particle that introduces exceptions. In this ayah, it shows that what follows (those who repent, believe, and do good deeds) are excluded from the previous statement. It has no root since it's a primary particle.
This is a special word that introduces a condition and refers to any person who does the actions mentioned after it. In this ayah, it refers to anyone who repents, believes, and does good deeds.

steps.m: to turn back or return (especially to Allah in repentance)

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif between first and second root letters to make it a hollow verb
Added alif after first letter to make the meaning causative - changing 'trust' to 'believe/have faith'
Added و at the beginning to connect this verb with تَابَ (repented) before it
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: and he did (connected to تَابَ and ءَامَنَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous actions of repenting and believing
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he became good'. The root ص-ل-ح gets vowels added to make it into a complete word.
Changes from a verb (action) to describing someone who has this quality
The word changed from 'righteous person' to 'righteous deed' by adding a special mark (ٰ) over the first letter and adding tanwin (-an) at the end to show it's the thing being done
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun that points to multiple people or things that are far away
Added the connecting hamza with kasra that helps join the next part
Added كَ at the end to show these things are far away, like saying 'over there'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before - in this case, it refers back to those who repented, believed, and did good deeds
Added optional pronunciation marks (small alif and wavy line) that don't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he entered) to present tense (he enters) by adding يَ at the start
This word describes what 'those people' (فَأُو۟لٰٓئِكَ) are doing - they are entering paradise

steps.m: to cover or hide from view

steps.n: Adding shadda makes the action stronger and more intense
Added ة to make it refer to a specific place that has this quality

steps.m: Paradise (the ultimate garden that is the object of يَدْخُلُونَ - they enter)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ to show it's the object being entered. This refers to Paradise because it's the ultimate garden that people will enter in the afterlife.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.m: and not (they will not be wronged in يُظْلَمُونَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This negates the verb يُظْلَمُونَ (they will be wronged) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show the action is being done to someone instead of by someone
Added ون to show that many people are being wronged, not just one person

steps.m: thing (that will not be wronged at all)
steps.n: Root letters ش-ي-أ become the noun 'thing' (شَيْء) by adding fatha and sukun marks. Used here to talk about how nothing will be wronged in Paradise.

steps.m: anything/at all (that will not be wronged)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha (ًا) to emphasize the complete negation - meaning 'not even the smallest thing' will be wronged
Added vowels and doubled the ن to show complete covering

steps.m: a garden (because it's covered with trees)
steps.n: Adding ة makes it a noun for a place that is covered with plants
Changed to plural form to show multiple gardens. This word is followed by عَدْنٍ (Eden) in the ayah to specify which gardens.
Added kasra to show this word owns or belongs to what comes after it (عَدْنٍ/Eden)
This is a proper noun referring to a specific garden or place of eternal dwelling
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks. This word describes the type of gardens (جَنَّاتِ) - gardens of eternal dwelling.
The tanween (double kasra) shows this word is being used to describe what kind of gardens these are
The base word تِي is written with ى instead of ي at the end. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added ٱل at the beginning which combines with ت to make ٱلَّت (the ل gets a shadda because it merges with the ت). This word connects back to جَنَّاتِ (gardens) to tell us more about them.

steps.m: to make a promise or commitment
he promised (subject of عِبَادَهُۥ - His servants)
steps.n: Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb. In this ayah, Allah is the one who promised something to His servants.

steps.m: The Most Merciful, full of mercy
steps.n: The ان ending makes it mean 'full of mercy'

steps.m: intensely merciful and kind (as the doer)
steps.n: Adds ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action, like saying 'the merciful one' is the doer

steps.m: The Most Merciful (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Adds ال (the) at the start, which causes the ر to get doubled (shown by ّ) because it's a special type of letter called a sun letter. This is one of Allah's names showing His perfect and complete mercy.
Changed from root meaning 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who worships' by adding vowels
Changes single servant into plural form showing multiple servants
Added هُۥ (his) with fatha connector to show possession. This word is what Allah promised (object of وَعَدَ)
Changes from an action to a noun - from 'being hidden' to 'the hidden/unseen'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific unseen realm rather than any unseen thing
The preposition بِ shows how something is done or experienced
The kasra ending shows this word follows the preposition بِ
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that His promise will definitely be fulfilled.

steps.m: indeed it (referring to وَعْدُهُۥ - His promise)
steps.n: Adds هُۥ which means 'it' and refers back to 'His promise' mentioned later in the ayah

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Added a-vowels (fatha) to each letter to make it past tense 'he existed'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.

steps.m: to promise, to make a commitment

steps.n: Changes from action to the thing itself (the promise). This word is the subject of كان (was) - it tells us what was being described as مأتيا (sure to come).
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action
Adds 'His' to show the promise belongs to Allah. This word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is about the thing or place where the action happens
Added shadda at the end to make the meaning stronger and more certain

steps.m: sure to come/arrive (as promised in وَعَدَ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to make it an adverb describing how the promise will definitely happen
Basic negation word that makes things negative
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added shadda to make the negation stronger. This word negates يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear), making it mean 'they do not hear at all'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he heard) to present tense (he hears) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they hear/listen (referring to the people in Paradise)
steps.n: Shows that multiple people in Paradise are doing the hearing/listening. This connects to فِيهَا (in it/Paradise) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ها which refers back to Paradise (جنة) mentioned earlier in the passage. This shows what is being talked about.

steps.m: to speak without purpose or benefit
useless talk (object of يَسْمَعُونَ - they hear)
steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'useless talk'. This is what they don't hear in Paradise.
Added ًا at the end to show this is what's being acted upon - they don't hear any useless talk in Paradise
This is the base form of the exception particle. It shows something is being excluded from what came before.

steps.m: except (what comes after from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) are added to strengthen and complete the exception meaning. In this ayah, it excludes 'peace' (سَلٰمًا) from the 'idle talk' (لَغْوًا) that won't be heard in Paradise.
Root letters س-ل-م got short 'a' vowels (فتحة) to make the word for 'peace'
Adding ا after ل makes it a noun meaning a complete state of peace
Added tanween alif to show this is something they will receive or experience in Paradise, as mentioned in the ayah
Added fatha to connect it to the next word
Added ل with fatha to show possession or benefit

steps.m: and for them (referring to the people of Paradise)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this belongs to a group of people (in this case, the people of Paradise being described)

steps.m: to provide with what is needed to live

steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'provide' into a simple noun meaning 'the thing provided'
The ُ (damma) ending shows this word is doing something - it's what they are receiving
Added هُمْ at the end to show this provision belongs to them. This connects to لَهُمْ (for them) earlier in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something
Added ها ('in it') to show this happens inside Paradise, which was mentioned before
The root relates to early morning or being early
Changed from a verb meaning 'being early' to a noun meaning 'early time' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to specify one instance of morning
Added tanween to show this describes when something happens
The root relates to darkness and evening time
Adding shadda makes it specifically mean a period of time (evening)
Adding ًا shows this word describes when something happens (they receive their provision in the evening)

steps.m: and in the evening (time when they receive رِزْقُهُمْ)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this with بُكْرَةً (morning) from the previous phrase, showing they receive their provision both morning and evening
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to feminine things. It's like saying 'that' when talking about something feminine.

steps.m: that (pointing to something far away, feminine) (refers to الْجَنَّةُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end makes it point to something far away. In this ayah, it's pointing to Paradise (الْجَنَّةُ) which is mentioned right after it.
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong/intense action
Added ة to make it a noun meaning 'a place that is surrounded/protected', which became used for 'garden'
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb نُورِثُ (we cause to inherit)

This is a relative pronoun that connects clauses when referring to feminine things. Here it refers back to Paradise (الْجَنَّةُ) which is feminine. It helps connect 'Paradise' to the description that follows about who will inherit it.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added hamza at start to show causing someone else to inherit
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and ُ at end. The weak letter و changes after نُ
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects 'we cause to inherit' (نُورِثُ) with 'our servants' (عِبَادِنَا).
Added a sukun (ْ) on the ن to show the word ends here. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'someone who serves'
Changed the vowels and added an alif to make it mean many servants

steps.m: our servants (owned by مِنْ عِبَادِنَا - from our servants)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show these are our servants. The ِ before نَا connects it smoothly. This word comes after مِنْ (from) in the ayah.

This is a special word that introduces a description of people. Here it means 'whoever' or 'the one who' and connects to كَانَ (was) to describe who will inherit Paradise - specifically those who were righteous.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of the word
The و drops and is replaced by ت at the start. The ي is doubled with shadda to show intensity of the quality. This pattern shows someone who consistently protects themselves from wrongdoing.
Adds tanween fatHa to show this word is describing the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' or 'not' depending on context. Here it means 'what' since it's the subject of نَتَنَزَّلُ (we descend).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda on middle letter shows the action happens multiple times or gradually
Adding تَ at beginning shows the action happens by itself or to oneself

steps.m: we descend gradually (in response to وَمَا)
steps.n: Adding نَ at beginning shows 'we' are doing the action, and ُ at end shows it's happening in the present

steps.m: except (what comes after is excluded from وَمَا نَتَنَزَّلُ - we do not descend)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows exception - it tells us that what comes after it is different or excluded from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it's saying the angels only descend by Allah's command, not any other way.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'a command'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ) in the ayah
Added بِ at the beginning to show this command is being used as a tool or means

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show this describes someone who does this action completely
Added kasra under the doubled letter to show this Lord belongs to someone
Added كَ at the end to show this is 'your' Lord. The word رَبِّ means Lord but adding كَ makes it 'your Lord'
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose

steps.m: for him, to him (referring to what is before and after us in أَيْدِينَا and خَلْفَنَا)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who the action is for. The ۥ is just a pronunciation mark. This word connects to what comes before and after us mentioned in the ayah.

This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that connects to the phrase 'before us' (بَيْنَ أَيْدِينَا). In this ayah, it's part of a series of things being described with مَا, listing 'what is before us, what is behind us, and what is between that.'

steps.m: to separate or be between things

steps.n: Added vowel marks to change this from a basic concept into a word showing location. This word is connected to أَيْدِينَا (our hands) in this ayah, showing what is between.
Forms the basic singular noun by adding fatha to first letter
Forms broken plural by adding hamza prefix and yaa infix
The kasra ending shows this word is being used as an object of a preposition
Adds 'our' to show possession and comes after بَيْنَ (between) in the ayah

steps.m: what/not (used here to mean 'not' before نَتَنَزَّلُ)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' or 'not' depending on how it's used. In this ayah, it's used with نَتَنَزَّلُ (we descend) to mean 'we do not descend'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'behind'
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of نَتَنَزَّلُ (we descend)
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'behind us'. This whole phrase is the object of the verb نَتَنَزَّلُ (we descend).
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' or 'not' depending on how it's used. In this ayah, it means 'not'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a common adverb showing location or relationship between things
Connected the letters and added vowels to make it work as a preposition. In this ayah, it's showing the relationship with أَيْدِينَا (before us).
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) but pronunciation stays the same
Adding لِ changes the meaning from pointing to something near to pointing to something far
Adding كَ reinforces that we're pointing to something far away
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to the previous part of the sentence. This word is connected to نَتَنَزَّلُ (we descend) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added basic past tense vowels

steps.m: he/it was (describing what رَبُّكَ was not)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter in past tense. This word describes رَبُّكَ (your Lord) in this ayah.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, or develop something to completion
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show someone who does this action perfectly
Added damma to show this is the doer of the action in relation to the verb كَانَ (was)
Added كَ to show this refers to your Lord, maintaining its role as the subject of كَانَ (was)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding ي with shadda makes it mean 'someone who forgets a lot'
Adding tanween alif makes this the object being forgotten and emphasizes its complete state of being forgotten

steps.m: to nurture, sustain, and develop something to completion
one who nurtures and sustains completely
steps.n: Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make 'lord/master' - someone who takes care of others
Added damma (ُ) at end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Root meaning 'rise up' becomes noun meaning 'sky' - like how the sky rises above us
Changes from 'sky' to 'skies' by adding ات at the end, with a small spelling change in the middle
Adding ال prefix makes the noun definite
Adding a double letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: of the mighty heavens (belonging to رَبُّ - the Lord)
steps.n: The ِ shows these heavens belong to رَبُّ (the Lord) who was mentioned right before this word
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun

steps.m: the earth (connected to رَّبُّ - Lord of)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the earth') and ِ to show it belongs to something (here showing it belongs to the Lord/رَّبُّ)

steps.m: and the earth (connected to ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect it with 'the heavens', making them a pair

steps.m: what (asking about or referring to things between السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: This is a particle used to ask about things or refer to general things. Here it's referring to everything between the heavens and earth.

steps.m: and what (connecting to رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: The وَ connects this word to the previous phrase about 'Lord of the heavens and earth', adding 'and what is between them'
Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added fatha at the end to show this word's relationship to what follows it

steps.m: between them both (referring to السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: Added هُمَا to show this refers to both heaven and earth mentioned earlier in the ayah
Command form made by adding ٱ at start and changing vowels - tells one person to worship
Added هُ at the end to show who is being worshipped. This هُ refers back to Allah (رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - Lord of the heavens and earth) mentioned in the previous ayah.
فَ means 'so' or 'then', showing this is a result of what came before
Adding the three fathas shows this is a complete past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding ٱ at start and changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
Added ط after ص to make the command stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: and be very patient! (connected to فَٱعْبُدْهُ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with the previous command 'worship Him' (فَٱعْبُدْهُ)
Added ة to transform from plural noun to abstract concept noun
Added pronoun ه to show possession (His worship). The kasra (ِ) before ه is needed because the word follows the preposition ل
Added لِ (for/to) at beginning to show this is the purpose of being patient (اصْطَبِرْ)
This is a question word that asks yes/no questions. Here it's asking about whether you know (تَعْلَمُ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you know (addressed to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The ت at the start shows this is happening now and the person doing it is 'you'. The sukun (ْ) on ل and damma (ُ) at the end are part of the present tense pattern. This word is part of a question 'Do you know...?'
This is a basic particle showing direction or purpose
Added هُ which means 'Him' and refers back to 'Lord' (رَبُّ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ۥ mark shows this is a long vowel sound.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb 'to name' into a describing word meaning 'named/equal' by adding a long 'ee' sound and doubling the last letter

steps.m: a namesake/equal (in status to someone else)
steps.n: Adds tanween (double marks) at the end to show it's indefinite - meaning 'a' namesake rather than 'the' namesake
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
The middle و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels - like changing 'said' to 'says'
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to be close and familiar with others

steps.n: Root means 'to be social' - changed vowels to make a noun meaning all humans/mankind
Changes from general 'mankind' to mean one specific human

steps.m: the human being (subject of يَقُولُ - he says)
steps.n: Added ال to mean 'the' and ُ at the end to show this is the one doing the action (saying) in this verse
This is the basic conditional/temporal particle meaning 'when'

steps.m: is it when? (questioning what follows in مَا مِتُّ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the start to make it a question word meaning 'is it when?'. The extra ء helps separate the two vowels to make it easier to say.
This is an interrogative particle used to ask questions. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'What! When I am dead...' expressing disbelief about resurrection.
The weak letter و changes to ي and is absorbed, causing the first vowel to change to kasra
Added ُّ (damma with shadda) at the end to show 'I' did the action. This word appears in a question where a person is asking if they will be brought forth alive after they have died.
This is the basic future particle that shows something will happen later. It's connected to أُخْرَجُ (I will be brought out) in this ayah.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing strong certainty about the future action. This emphasizes that the person will definitely be brought out alive.
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he exited'
Changed from 'he exited' to 'he was forced out' by making it passive - adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show it's in the nominative case
Root letters combined to make 'alive' - middle letter has shadda (double letter)
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence. This relates to أُخْرَجُ (I will be brought out) in the ayah, describing the state of being brought out.
This letter turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Adds 'and' to connect this question with the previous thought
Adds negation to make this a rhetorical question expressing surprise that someone doesn't remember. This introduces the next verb يَذْكُرُ (remember/mention).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يَ at start to show 'he' in present, 2) putting sukun on first root letter, 3) changing middle vowel to damma, 4) adding final damma to show it's doing the action. This word comes after أَوَلَا (and does not) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be close and familiar with others

steps.n: Root meaning 'being social' became a noun meaning 'humans' (since humans are social beings)
Adding ان changes it from humans as a group to a single human being
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and changed alif to alif with bar for writing style

steps.m: the human being (subject of يَذْكُرُ - remembers/mentions)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this is the one doing the action in the sentence
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows it

steps.m: that we (subject of خَلَقْنٰهُ - we created him)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'we' to show who is being talked about. This connects to the next word خَلَقْنٰهُ (we created him) where 'we' is doing the creating.
Added fatha (َ) after each letter to make the basic past tense verb. This shows the action happened in the past.

steps.m: we created (referring to Allah using the royal 'We')
steps.n: Added نٰ at the end to show Allah is speaking using the royal 'We'. The ْ (sukun) was added before نٰ to help pronunciation.

steps.m: We created him (referring to the human mentioned as الْإِنسٰنُ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who was created. This refers back to 'the human' (الْإِنسٰنُ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts from. In this ayah, it connects 'We created him' (خَلَقْنٰهُ) with 'before' (قَبْلُ) to show when the creation happened.
Added vowels (َ and ْ) to the root letters to make the word mean 'before' in time
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of يَكُ (was) in this ayah
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it past tense
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negative statement to what came before
Added يَ at start to show 'he' is doing it now, and added ُ vowels to show it's happening
Removed the long vowel و to make the word simpler
Forms a noun meaning 'a thing' from the concept of existence. This word follows يَكُ (was) as its predicate.
The word gets an -an ending (ًا) to show it completes the meaning of يَكُ (was). The little hamza (ء) changes shape because of this ending.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares for others perfectly
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to something
Added كَ (ka) at the end to show this is your Lord
Added وَ (wa) at start to mean 'and'
Added فَ at the start to connect this oath to what came before it, making it stronger

steps.m: to gather or collect together in one place

steps.n: The ن shows that 'we' are doing the action
Adding هُمْ at the end shows who is being gathered
The نَّ at the end makes it emphatic - like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
The لَ at the start combines with نَّ at the end to show strong emphasis and future tense
Adding the object pronoun هُمْ (them) to specify who is being gathered

steps.m: devil, one who is rebelliously distant
steps.n: Adds يا between letters to make a noun meaning 'one who does this action' - like turning 'rebel' into 'rebel person'
Adds ين to make the word refer to multiple devils/rebellious ones
Adds ال with shadda on following letter to make the noun definite

steps.m: and the devils (object of لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ - we will gather them)
steps.n: Adds conjunction و meaning 'and' and final fatḥa as this word is the object of the verb لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ (we will gather them)
Removing the ـَ (fatha) ending mark while keeping the و (and) prefix
Starting with the basic form of this connecting word
Added the damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects 'We will gather them' with 'We will bring them' to show these actions happen in sequence.
Adding أ at the start makes the verb causative - meaning someone makes something happen
Adding نُ changes it to 'we' form in present tense
The لَ at the start and نَّ at the end work together to add strong emphasis, meaning 'most certainly/definitely'

steps.m: we will certainly make them present (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adds the object pronoun 'them' (masculine plural) referring back to the disbelievers mentioned in the previous verses
Root letters got vowels to make a word meaning 'around' - like a circle around something
Added fatha at the end to show this word is owned by or connected to Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) in the sentence
This is a proper noun that doesn't come from an Arabic root - it's the name of Hell. The base word is believed to come from Hebrew 'Gehinnom'.
Added fatha vowels and shadda to show correct pronunciation. The shadda on ن shows it's pronounced twice (ja-han-nam).

steps.m: Hell (as the object being surrounded in حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being surrounded in the phrase 'around Hell' (حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ)

steps.m: to kneel or sit on knees

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to kasra and added shadda to make it a noun describing the condition of kneeling

steps.m: kneeling intensely (as the state they were brought in)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes the state they were in when they were brought around Hell (refers back to لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ in the ayah)

steps.m: then (connects to لَنَنزِعَنَّ - we will surely remove)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows sequence of events. It connects to the next statement about removing people, showing this happens after previous events. The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger to emphasize the sequence.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense base form
Adds the first person plural prefix to make it 'we'
Adding لَ makes it emphatic, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'

steps.m: surely we will pull out (from كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ - every sect)
steps.n: Adding emphatic نَّ makes the statement a very strong assertion. This word connects to what follows in the ayah - 'from every sect'

steps.m: from (showing which ones will be taken out of شِيعَةٍ/group)
steps.n: Basic particle showing something is taken or selected from a larger group. In this ayah, it shows which people will be taken from each group.

steps.m: from (showing which ones will be taken out of شِيعَةٍ/group)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word connects to what comes after it, like saying 'from the' in English
Basic meaning of completeness or totality
Adds damma and kasra vowel marks with shadda to create the word meaning 'every'. This word is describing شِيعَةٍ (group) in this ayah.
Basic meaning of following or spreading
Changed the root 'to follow' into 'followers' by adding vowel marks - like turning 'follow' into 'follower' in English. Links to كُلِّ (every/each) to mean 'every group of followers'
Added ة at the end to show we're talking about one specific group
Added ٍ to show this word comes after مِن (from) in مِن كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ (from each group)
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of أَشَدُّ (most intense)
Added هم at the end to show we're asking about 'which one of them'. This word is asking which of them is أَشَدُّ (most intense) against the Most Merciful.
Basic meaning of strength or intensity
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Combined the doubled letters with a shadda to make an adjective meaning 'strong'
Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative, showing this group is 'more strong' in rebellion than others
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or above something

steps.m: upon, against, over (referring to ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ - the Most Merciful)
steps.n: Adding ى changes this from a word meaning 'high' into a word meaning 'upon/against'. Here it shows someone opposing Allah, like standing against Him.
Basic meaning of mercy and compassion

steps.m: The Most Merciful, full of mercy
steps.n: The ان ending makes it mean 'full of mercy'
The shadda intensifies the meaning further

steps.m: The Most Merciful (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Adding ال causes the first letter to get a shadda. This is a special name of Allah showing His perfect and complete mercy.
Added -i sound at end to show it belongs with another word
Basic meaning of rebellion or disobedience
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to kasra and added doubled ي to show intense quality
Added ًا to show this describes how something is done, connecting to أَشَدُّ (most severe) in the ayah

steps.m: then/next/afterwards (connecting to لَنَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ - 'We know best')
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to show this is a strong connecting word that introduces what happens next. In this ayah, it connects to what Allah knows best about.
Basic pronoun meaning 'we' - this is the subject (doer) of the verb أَعْلَمُ (know better/most knowing) in this ayah
The لَ at the start adds emphasis to show strong certainty, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'. This emphasizes that Allah is stating with absolute certainty that 'we know better'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: more knowing (than the others mentioned in بِٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Changed to comparative form to show 'more/most knowing'. This word is comparing knowledge levels with those mentioned after it in the verse.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who' or 'which' when talking about one person or thing
Adding ين makes it talk about many people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it point to specific people we're talking about. The ّ (shadda) makes the ل sound stronger.

steps.m: with/by those who (connected to أَعْلَمُ - most knowing)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows this word is connected to أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) in the ayah, telling us who or what is known
Basic pronoun used to refer to a group of people. In this ayah, it refers to the object of the verb أَعْلَمُ (we know), meaning 'we know them'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed from regular verb 'to be close' into comparative form 'more/most' by adding أ at start and changing ending to ى - like adding 'er' in English to make 'closer'
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show proper pronunciation. This word connects to بِهَا (of it) in the ayah.
Basic pronoun for referring to something feminine

steps.m: with/by her/it (referring to Hell/Fire mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how something is done. In this ayah, it refers to being burned with/by the Fire.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes to make a word mean 'very intense burning' by doubling the last letter and changing vowels

steps.m: burning intensely (as a state or condition)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatHa to make the word indefinite accusative

steps.m: if/whether (introduces the condition 'any of you' in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the hypothetical situation of 'any of you' (مِّنكُمْ) that follows in the ayah

steps.m: and if (connects to previous statement while introducing the condition 'any of you')
steps.n: The و connects this conditional statement to what came before, while إِن introduces the condition about 'any of you' (مِّنكُمْ) that follows

steps.m: from (showing the starting point of وَارِدُهَا - those who will enter it)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it connects to وَارِدُهَا (those who will enter it) to show which group they come from.

steps.m: from you all (showing which group وَارِدُهَا - those who will enter it - come from)
steps.n: The كُمْ suffix is added to indicate 'you all' as the object of the preposition

steps.m: from you all (showing which group وَارِدُهَا - those who will enter it - come from)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) is added on the م for pronunciation because it comes after وَإِن
This is the basic form of the word that means 'except'. It's a special word that doesn't come from a root - it's used to show that something is different from what was mentioned before.

steps.m: except (from وَإِن مِّنكُمْ - and there is not among you)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it's excluding something from the previous statement 'and there is not among you'.
These are the basic letters that give us the core meaning of arriving or approaching
Added alif after first letter and vowels to make it mean 'someone who does the action'
Added damma to show this is doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: the one arriving to it (subject of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what is being approached. This word is doing the action in the verb كَانَ (was).
This is the basic past tense form
Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic for weak middle letter verbs.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or about something else. It connects to رَبِّكَ (your Lord) to show whose decision this is.
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing style that shows this is a permanent form of the word. It doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show complete mastery and authority in nurturing/caring
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something (in this case, by 'you')
Added ك (ka) at the end to mean 'your'. Shows this is 'your Lord' rather than just 'Lord'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun. The fatha-sukun pattern makes it mean the result of the action.
Adding ًا changes it from a noun to an adverb, showing how something is done. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the decree mentioned is definitely going to happen.

steps.m: to decide or settle something definitively
steps.n: This root carries the basic meaning of making a final, binding decision
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show this is something that was decided, not the one doing the deciding
Added shadda to show this is absolutely and completely decided

steps.m: decreed (as a matter that is settled)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how something is - in this case, how the matter mentioned before is definitely decreed

steps.m: then (connects to نُنَجِّى - we will save)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to complete the word. This word connects two ideas, showing that the second thing happens after the first. Here it connects to the word نُنَجِّى (we will save), showing what happens next in the sequence.

steps.m: to rescue or deliver from harm

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this saving is done thoroughly or multiple times

steps.m: we rescue (those who were mindful of Allah)
steps.n: Added نُ at the start to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوا۟ (those who were mindful) who are being rescued.
This is a special demonstrative word composed of: ٱلْ (definite article) + ذِي (who/which) + نَ (plural suffix). The lām gets a shadda when connected to ذِي.

steps.m: to protect oneself or others from harm

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb 'he protected' by adding vowel marks
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The و disappeared because it came between vowels.
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action

steps.m: we leave behind (subject of the sentence after ثُمَّ)
steps.n: Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and ُ at the end to show it's happening now or in the future. This continues the action after ثُمَّ (then) from the previous phrase.

steps.m: and we leave behind (connected to نُنَجِّى/we save)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous verb نُنَجِّى (we save), showing these actions happen in sequence: first we save some people, then we leave others behind

steps.m: and we leave behind (connected to نُنَجِّى/we save)
steps.n: Added shadda on و for emphasis in pronunciation when connecting with previous word

steps.m: to do wrong, to be unfair

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense verb meaning 'he did wrong'
Changes from verb 'he did wrong' to noun meaning 'one who does wrong'
Makes the word plural to mean 'multiple wrongdoers'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Adds the accusative case ending
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'
Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it' where 'it' refers to something feminine (like Hell/النار which was mentioned earlier in the context)

steps.m: to kneel down, to fall on one's knees

steps.n: Changed the word to describe the state or manner of kneeling

steps.m: fallen on their knees (describing their state in Hell)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this word describe HOW the wrongdoers will be - on their knees in Hell. Like adding -ly in English to describe how something happens.

steps.m: when (introduces what happens next in تُتْلَىٰ)
steps.n: This is a time particle that introduces what happens when our verses are recited. It sets up a condition or sequence of events.

steps.m: and when (introduces what happens next in تُتْلَىٰ)
steps.n: The و connects this new event (verses being recited) to what happened before. When you see وَإِذَا, it usually means 'and then when' or 'and at the time when' something happens.

steps.m: to recite or follow in sequence

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form by adding fathas and turning final و into ا
Changes from active (he recited) to passive (it is recited)

steps.m: it is being recited (of آيَاتُنَا - our verses)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show this is a formal written form. The word refers to آيَاتُنَا (our verses) being recited.
This is a basic preposition showing something being on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to what is being recited.
The ى changes to يْ to connect with the next part. This is just a spelling change to make the words fit together.

steps.m: upon them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned later as الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' for a group of males. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers who are mentioned later.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: The fatha vowel helps form the singular noun 'sign/verse'
Adding نا means 'our' - shows these are Allah's signs/verses
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added 'our' and showed this is what is being recited in the verse
Added shadda to strengthen the middle letter, making the meaning stronger
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple clear proofs. This word describes ءَايٰتُنَا (our verses) in this ayah.
Added tanween kasr (ٍ) to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence. Also added the elongated alif (ٰ) for proper spelling.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone (he) did this action in the past.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the ذ letter. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in this ayah - it tells us who did the disbelieving.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb for a single person
Changed the word to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to multiple people. It points to the believers (who are mentioned right after this word with ءَامَنُوٓا۟)

steps.m: for those who (connected to خَيْرٌ - better)

steps.m: and they worked/acted/did (righteous deeds) (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect this word with ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) in describing those who both believe and do good deeds
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he was good' (verb) to 'someone who is good' (describing a person)
Makes the word feminine plural to refer to multiple good deeds or actions
Adding ال makes it refer to specific righteous deeds (object of عَمِلُوا۟ - they did)
The kasra ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb عَمِلُوا۟ (they did)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he will make or set (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future. This word is the main verb telling what the Most Merciful (الرَّحْمٰنُ) will do.
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added هُم (meaning 'them') to show who it's for
Added damma at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ (the Most Merciful)
Changes the word into an intensive adjective showing complete mercy
Added ال at the beginning to mean 'the' and doubled the ر letter to help pronunciation
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: to love deeply, to show affection

steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: deep love, affection (as the object being granted by الرَّحْمٰنُ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being granted by Allah (الرَّحْمٰنُ) in the verse
Base emphatic particle that forms the core of this compound
Adds sequential/consequential meaning

steps.m: so/then only (referring to يَسَّرْنٰهُ - We have made it easy)
steps.n: The ما here restricts the meaning to show that the Quran was made easy ONLY for the specific purpose mentioned after (to give good news to the righteous and warn the stubborn). This particle connects to the verb يَسَّرْنٰهُ (We have made it easy) that follows it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the س to emphasize the act of making something easy
The suffix نا indicates 'we' as the subject of the verb
Adding هُ shows what was made easy (referring to the Quran)
Added vowels and alif to make the basic noun form for 'tongue' or 'language'
Added preposition بِ (with) at the beginning of the word

steps.m: with your tongue (related to يَسَّرْنٰهُ - We made easy)
steps.n: Added ِ before the pronoun كَ (your) because it's being possessed, and added كَ to show it belongs to you. This word is connected to يَسَّرْنٰهُ (We made easy) in the previous part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding the shadda (double letter) changes it from 'he brought news' to 'give good news!' - like making it a stronger command
Adding تُ at the beginning shows that 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: so that you give good news (to ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this is happening for a purpose - to give good news to the God-conscious people (ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ)
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or used

steps.m: with/by it (referring to لِسَانِكَ - your tongue)
steps.n: Added هِ which means 'it' to show what is being used. In this ayah, it refers back to لِسَانِكَ (your tongue) mentioned earlier.
Added fathas to make basic past tense verb

Changed from a basic verb (to protect) into a noun meaning 'one who protects themselves'. The م and ت at the start combine with the original و to create مُتَّ, showing someone actively guarding against wrong
Added ين to make it plural

steps.m: the God-fearing ones (object of لِتُبَشِّرَ - so that you may give glad tidings to)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and َ to show it's the object of the verb لِتُبَشِّرَ (to give glad tidings to)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he warned' to 'you warn' by adding تُ prefix

steps.m: and that you warn (connected to لِتُبَشِّرَ - so that you give good news)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect it to 'give good news', showing both are goals or purposes
Added kasra (ِ) to connect with next word

steps.m: with/by/in it (referring to لِسَانِكَ - your tongue)
steps.n: Added هِ to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to 'your tongue' mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added small ۦ mark after هِ to help with proper Quranic recitation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to stand firm, rise up, establish

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' - those who stand together as a community. This word is receiving the warning in the ayah.
Added ًا to show this refers to any people in general, not a specific group. This word is receiving the warning mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: to argue stubbornly, to be extremely difficult
extremely stubborn ones (object of وَتُنذِرَ - and you warn)
steps.n: The root letters ل-د-د became لُّدًّا (luddan) - meaning stubborn people - with extra marks to show it's receiving the action of warning
This is a question word used to ask about quantity

steps.m: and how many (connects to أَهْلَكْنَا - We destroyed)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to the verb that follows (أَهْلَكْنَا - We destroyed), showing that this is an exclamation about the many nations that were destroyed
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes someone cause the action instead of just doing it
Adding نَا at the end shows that 'we' did the action
This root relates to something coming before something else
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added هُم at the end to show this is happening before them (the generations mentioned later in the ayah)
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or that something is part of a larger group
The م is doubled (gets shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in ن

steps.m: generation (a group of people connected by time)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning a group connected by time
The tanween shows this is one of many generations being discussed in the context of destruction of previous peoples
This is an interrogative particle that has no root since it is a non-derived word (حرف)
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: a fatha (َ) on ه and a sukun (ْ) on ل
The root relates to feeling or perceiving something
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Combined the repeated س with a shadda
Added تُ at start to show 'you/she' and changed middle vowel to make it present tense
Added a 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action, connected to 'hears' in this ayah
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The ِ and ْ marks help with pronunciation.

steps.m: from them (referring to the generations mentioned in قَرْنٍ)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' (masculine plural). This refers back to the previous generations (قَرْنٍ) mentioned in the verse.

steps.m: from/of/among (shows where something starts or what it belongs to)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows the starting point or source of something
The ن merged into م making a stronger م sound. This happens when مِنْ comes before certain letters. Here it connects with قَرْنٍ (generation).
Connected the root letters together with fatha to form the basic word
Added the tanween kasra at the end to show this word is indefinite and is being acted upon by the verb تُحِسُّ (you perceive)

This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between two options. Here it connects 'you perceive' (تُحِسُّ) with 'you hear' (تَسْمَعُ), asking if you can either perceive or hear any trace of them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The تَ at start shows 'you' and the ُ at end shows it's happening now. The سْ has a sukun to make it easier to pronounce.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or purpose. In this ayah, it connects to the word رِكْزًۢا (sound/whisper) to show who the sound is for.

steps.m: for/to them (masculine plural, referring to the destroyed generations)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show we're talking about multiple people (specifically the destroyed generations mentioned earlier in the ayah)

steps.m: to make a faint sound or whisper
steps.n: This root relates to making sounds or fixing something in place
The vowel pattern changes this from an action into a noun describing the actual sound
The ًا ending makes it 'a faint sound' that is being heard - like when you say 'I heard a whisper' instead of just the word 'whisper'
